data.task:
ANALYSIS-ONLY OWNER MAP (read-only — you MUST NOT change any product code, tests, or governance files; the ONLY file you write is the new analysis doc named below). Context: founder-accepted ADR-0026 (cocoder/decisions/0026-onboard-existing-as-oscar-priority.md) pivots the existing-repo onboarding audit from a standalone phase-executor runner-mode to an ordinary Oscar-driven priority, and renames 'Takeover' -> 'Onboard (existing repo)'. Before we rebuild, we need a precise owner map so the rebuild touches the right surfaces and does not churn code slated for deletion.

Produce a single new markdown file: docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md. Do not edit anything else.

The doc must contain, with file:line evidence for every claim (cite the actual file and line ranges you read — do not assert from memory):

1. RETIRE list — every code unit that implements the standalone executor RUNNER-MODE that ADR-0026 supersedes: the executor.ts phase cursor, the 'awaiting-founder' status + typed-resume gate mechanism, and the bespoke executor gate semantics. For each: the file, the exported symbols, and what currently calls it.

2. KEEP-AS-TOOLING list — the audit machinery ADR-0026 explicitly preserves and the Oscar-driven flow will reuse: the deep-read Play, p2-fanout / p2-dispatch, p3-cross-check, recon / recon-pass, intent / intent-artifacts, estimate, p4-questions, p5-synthesis, p6-apply, and any render/input helpers. For each: file, exported symbols, and CRITICALLY — what currently invokes it. Flag any unit whose ONLY caller is the retired executor (i.e. it becomes dead/orphaned code once the executor is deleted, and the rebuild must re-wire a caller or it dies).

3. KEEP-AS-IS (orthogonal) list — units that are independent of the driver and stay unchanged: the scaffold primitive (scaffoldCocoderZone), the cocoder/**-only audit write-boundary (gate.ts auditWriteBoundary + AuditWriteBoundaryError), and the loader's daemon discovery surface (loadOnboardingPlaybooks / the GET .../priorities 'onboarding' field). Confirm with evidence whether each truly does NOT depend on the retired executor.

4. RENAME inventory — a table of EVERY occurrence of the identifiers 'cocoder-takeover' (the playbook id) and the word 'takeover' (case-insensitive) across packages/** and cocoder/**, each row classified as: (a) RETIRE (lives in code being deleted — do NOT rename, it goes away), (b) RENAME (lives on a surviving surface and must change to onboard-existing / 'Onboard (existing repo)'), or (c) HISTORICAL (session-log / past-ADR narrative record that should stay as-written for provenance). Include the playbook skeleton file packages/personas/base/playbooks/cocoder-takeover.md (which renames to onboard-existing.md) and every test that pins the id/word. Start from: git ls-files | grep -i takeover, plus grep -rliE 'takeover' packages cocoder, plus grep -rn 'cocoder-takeover'.

5. DEPENDENCY EDGES / break-risk — list the edges where deleting the RETIRE set would break the build if the RENAME/KEEP wiring is not updated in lockstep (e.g. loader parses the skeleton's baked phase table that only the executor consumes; launcher.ts createDaemonPlaybookPhaseAction composes P1..P6; daemon mutations.test.ts e2e drives the executor; tests that assert 'awaiting-founder'). This is the section that tells the rebuild what must move together.

Acceptance criteria: docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md exists; every RETIRE/KEEP/RENAME claim carries a real file:line citation; the rename table accounts for ALL takeover occurrences from the greps above (none silently dropped); section 2 explicitly names any unit orphaned by the executor deletion; section 5 lists the lockstep break edges. Must-not-break: zero changes to any file other than the new doc (the working tree diff is exactly one added file). Run no build/test changes. If a grep reveals an occurrence you cannot confidently classify, list it under an explicit 'UNCLASSIFIED — needs Oscar judgment' subsection rather than guessing.

data.task:
REBUILD ATOM 1 — Remove the daemon-side executor DRIVER (ADR-0026: existing-repo onboarding becomes an ordinary Oscar-driven priority; founder decisions this run: (a) Oscar atoms call the kept core engines directly — drop the executor + phase protocol; (b) rename rides bundled with each retire atom, so here the takeover identifiers on this surface are DELETED with the code, not renamed). Use docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md as the map; cite it.

Dependency order matters: the daemon CONSUMES core's executor, so the daemon driver must be removed FIRST (this atom) to keep the build green; core executor.ts deletion is a LATER atom — do NOT touch it here.

DELETE (daemon side only):
- The `target.kind === 'playbook'` launch branch in `launchRun` (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~597-628): the branch that creates an onboarding-playbook run, calls startPlaybookExecutor, records the `playbook-executor` event, and sets run status `awaiting-founder`.
- `createDaemonPlaybookPhaseAction` and its whole composition (launcher.ts ~306-385): the P1->P6 phase-action wiring, deep-read/dispatch wiring, top-tier resolver binding, the P7 apply hook (~366-384), and the takeover-keyed post-wrap support audit-boundary hook (~878-889 / labels at ~379-381,888). These are the executor path and go away.
- The playbook-target acceptance in `POST /runs` (packages/daemon/src/routes.ts ~78-116): revert the route to accept `priorityId` only (remove the exactly-one-of playbook branch and any playbookId request plumbing in the route).
- The daemon executor end-to-end tests in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts (the `playbookId: 'cocoder-takeover'` / `awaiting-founder` / manual loadPlaybookExecutor-resume / P7-apply cases, ~665-935) and any read-surface/route test assertions that depend on launching a playbook. The Oscar-driven flow gets its OWN proof in a later atom (ADR-0026 retires the executor proof) — removing this coverage now is correct, not a regression.

KEEP (do NOT touch in this atom):
- core `packages/core/src/playbooks/executor.ts` and its re-exports (deleted in the NEXT atom — leaving it imported by nothing-in-daemon is fine; core's own executor.test.ts still covers it so core stays green).
- ALL the kept core engines and their unit tests (recon, recon-pass, intent, intent-artifacts, estimate, p2-fanout, p2-dispatch, p3-cross-check, p4-questions, p5-synthesis, p6-apply, and the runPlaybookP*Action / render / input helpers) — untouched.
- The scaffold primitive (scaffoldCocoderZone) and the workspace-creation scaffold path in routes.ts (~340-346) — untouched.
- The audit write-boundary CORE (commit-gate/gate.ts AuditWriteBoundary / AuditWriteBoundaryError / runCommitGate) — untouched and still exported; only the daemon takeover hook that APPLIED it is deleted (the new flow re-adds an application later).
- The loader discovery surface (loadOnboardingPlaybooks, the daemon `onboarding` field, priority-order.ts mode mapping) — leave AS-IS this atom; its removal/reshaping is a later atom.
- The store `Run.playbookId` column / schema / migrations — do NOT change them this atom (leaving a nullable unused column is harmless). If removing the launcher playbook branch forces a `LaunchRunTarget`/store TYPE change to satisfy typecheck, make the MINIMAL launcher-local type adjustment to keep the build green, but do NOT alter store schema/migrations; if you cannot keep typecheck green without a store-schema change, STOP and report the exact blocker rather than expanding scope.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA / VERIFY (run these and paste output):
- `pnpm -w typecheck` clean.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` green (with the executor e2e cases removed; all ORDINARY-priority-run daemon tests still pass unchanged).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green and UNCHANGED count (you touched no core files).
- `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` green.
MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary priority runs (POST /runs with priorityId, launchRun priority branch, the Oscar<->Bob loop, scaffold-on-workspace-create) must behave exactly as before — show a daemon test proving an ordinary priority run still launches. Do NOT touch core executor.ts, the engines, the scaffold, or gate.ts core. The working-tree diff should be confined to packages/daemon/** (launcher.ts, routes.ts, the two daemon test files). If you find a daemon reference to the executor you must delete to stay green that is NOT listed above, delete it and note it; if it would force touching core or the store schema, STOP and report.

data.reason: Daemon executor driver removed (launch branch, phase-action composition, P7/awaiting-founder hooks, playbook route target); diff confined to packages/daemon/** with core/scaffold/gate untouched (git diff packages/core empty); typecheck + topology + core(373) + daemon(211) all green; ordinary priority/ticket runs unchanged (playbookOnly now 400).

REBUILD ATOM 2 — Delete the core executor + its phase-protocol wrappers, keeping the pure engines (ADR-0026; the daemon driver was removed in atom 1, so core can now drop the executor without going red). Use docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md as the map. The engines stay so the Oscar-driven flow (later atoms) can call them directly as plain library functions.

DELETE:
- The whole file packages/core/src/playbooks/executor.ts (the phase cursor, playbook-state.json persistence, awaiting-founder pause, typed-resume, and the types PlaybookPhaseAction / PlaybookPhaseActionInput / PlaybookGateState / PlaybookExecutorState/Status / FounderApproval / Start|Resume|LoadPlaybookExecutorInput / PlaybookExecutorResult / LoadedPlaybookExecutor / PlaybookExecutorDeps, and startPlaybookExecutor / resumePlaybookExecutor / loadPlaybookExecutor / readPlaybookExecutorState).
- The whole file packages/core/tests/playbook-executor.test.ts.
- The SIX executor-protocol wrapper functions (they are pure adapters over the engines and were only ever called by the now-deleted daemon composition): createPlaybookPhaseAction (p1-action.ts:41), createPlaybookP2PhaseAction (p2-action.ts:41), createPlaybookP3PhaseAction (p3-action.ts:110), createPlaybookP4PhaseAction (p4-action.ts:28), createPlaybookP5PhaseAction (p5-action.ts:29), createPlaybookP6PhaseAction (p6-apply.ts:62) — plus each file's `import type { ... PlaybookPhaseAction ... } from './executor.js'` line.
- The two executor-gate adapter helpers that depend on PlaybookGateState: approvalFromP6Gate (p6-apply.ts:124) and founderCheckpointFromGate (p5-action.ts:69). The Oscar-driven flow will supply founder input through a different (non-executor) path in a later atom.
- The executor re-exports AND the createPlaybook*PhaseAction re-exports from packages/core/src/playbooks/index.ts and packages/core/src/index.ts (keep the runPlaybookP*Action / engine / type re-exports).
- In packages/core/tests/playbook-p6-apply.test.ts: remove ONLY the test cases that exercise approvalFromP6Gate (the deleted gate adapter); keep all applyP6Governance / runPlaybookP6Action cases.

KEEP — UNCHANGED BEHAVIOR (do NOT weaken or delete these; the rebuild calls them directly):
- All engine entry points: runPlaybookP1Action / runPlaybookP2Action / runPlaybookP3Action / runPlaybookP4Action / runPlaybookP5Action / runPlaybookP6Action, applyP6Governance, and the pure engines they use (inventoryRepo, runAgenticRecon, runIntentIntake, enumerateIntentArtifacts, buildEstimate, runDeepReadSource/combineSourcePair, resolveDeepReadAssignments/createDeepReadTurn, buildRound, buildFounderQuestions, synthesizeP5Governance) plus all p*-input.ts / p*-render.ts helpers and their unit tests.
- runPlaybookP5Action MUST retain its `founderCheckpoint?: P5FounderCheckpoint | null = null` parameter (P5FounderCheckpoint is defined in p5-synthesis.ts, NOT executor — it stays). applyP6Governance MUST retain its local P6FounderApproval-typed signature. You are only removing the executor-gate ADAPTERS that fed these, not the parameters themselves.
- loader.ts (including the OnboardingPlaybookPhase type and the baked phase-table parsing) — UNTOUCHED this atom (its reshaping is atom 3).
- scaffold (scaffold.ts) and the commit-gate core (commit-gate/gate.ts, AuditWriteBoundary) — UNTOUCHED.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA / VERIFY (run and paste output):
- `pnpm -w typecheck` clean — this is the completeness guard: it must catch every dangling import of a deleted executor symbol. If it flags an importer NOT named above, decouple it with the SAME principle (keep the engine, delete the executor adapter/import); if decoupling it cleanly is impossible without changing an engine's behavior or touching loader/scaffold/gate/daemon, STOP and report the exact file + line.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green (executor test file removed; ALL engine/input/render unit tests still pass with the same assertions — do not edit an engine test to make it pass).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` green and UNCHANGED (daemon no longer imports any deleted symbol after atom 1; you should not need to touch daemon at all — if you do, STOP and report).
- `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` green.
MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary priority runs; the kept engines keep identical signatures/behavior except for the removed executor-gate adapters. The working-tree diff must be confined to packages/core/** (the deleted executor.ts + deleted playbook-executor.test.ts, the six action files, the two index files, and the p6-apply test trim). Do NOT touch loader.ts, scaffold, gate.ts, or any daemon file.

REBUILD ATOM 3 (A1) — Retire the onboarding DISCOVERY surface (founder decision this run, Option A: onboarding becomes an ordinary scaffold-seeded priority; loadOnboardingPlaybooks + the daemon 'onboarding' field retire; this amends ADR-0020 §7). The playbook LAUNCH surface is already gone (atom 1) and the executor is gone (atom 2), so this discovery surface is now vestigial. Use docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md §3 (Loader discovery surface) as the map.

DELETE:
- The whole file packages/core/src/playbooks/loader.ts (it is ENTIRELY onboarding discovery: loadOnboardingPlaybooks, the baked-phase-table parser, and the Onboarding* / PlaybookPhaseKind / OnboardingPlaybookPhaseId types). Atom 2 already severed every engine from these (the only remaining references are the index re-exports below), so nothing in the kept engines needs them.
- The loader re-exports from packages/core/src/playbooks/index.ts (the `from './loader.js'` block: loadOnboardingPlaybooks, OnboardingPlaybook, OnboardingPlaybookMode, OnboardingPlaybookPhase, OnboardingPlaybookPhaseId, PlaybookPhaseKind) and the matching re-exports from packages/core/src/index.ts.
- packages/core/tests/playbooks.test.ts (it tests loadOnboardingPlaybooks + phase parsing). If it contains any test NOT about the loader, trim only the loader cases instead and say so; otherwise delete the file.
- The daemon 'onboarding' summary in packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts (the OnboardingSummary type + the loadOnboardingPlaybooks->onboarding mapping, ~lines 15-31) and its use in packages/daemon/src/routes.ts GET /workspaces/:id/priorities (~lines 354-359) so the response no longer carries an `onboarding` field.
- The onboarding/cocoder-takeover assertions in packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts (the lines asserting the `onboarding` field includes the takeover playbook, ~180-187). Keep the rest of that test (ordinary priorities surface) intact.

KEEP / DO NOT TOUCH:
- The shipped skeleton markdown files packages/personas/base/playbooks/*.md (new-primary.md, cocoder-takeover.md, drift-audit.md, new-primary-tech-stack.md, README.md) STAY ON DISK this atom — they become scaffold-seed priority templates in the NEXT atom (A2). They will simply be unreferenced by code after this atom; that is expected and fine. Do not delete or rename them here.
- The kept engines (runPlaybookP*Action, applyP6Governance, all pure engines + input/render helpers) and their tests — untouched.
- scaffold (scaffold.ts) and the commit-gate core (gate.ts) — untouched.
- If `basePlaybooksDir` (from @cocoder/personas) is now unused after removing its last caller, you may leave the export in place (harmless); only remove a dangling IMPORT if typecheck flags it.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA / VERIFY (run and paste output):

The working-tree diff should be confined to: packages/core/src/playbooks/loader.ts (deleted), packages/core/src/playbooks/index.ts, packages/core/src/index.ts, packages/core/tests/playbooks.test.ts (deleted/trimmed), packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts. If you must touch a file outside this set to keep the build green, do the minimal change and note it; if it would mean editing UI components or the scaffold, STOP and report.

data.reason: Onboarding discovery surface retired (Option A): loader.ts + loader re-exports + daemon 'onboarding' field (priority-order.ts/routes.ts) + playbooks.test.ts deleted, read-surfaces onboarding assertions trimmed; diff confined to the named set, engines/scaffold/gate untouched, skeleton .md kept for A2; typecheck + topology green, core 367 (only playbooks.test.ts's 4 tests gone) + daemon 211 green.

data.task:
REBUILD ATOM 4 (A2) — Transform the retired Takeover skeleton into an ordinary onboard-existing PRIORITY, and bundle the live doc renames. This is content/markdown only (no code, no build behavior change). Founder decision this run (Option A): onboarding is an ordinary scaffold-seeded priority; the executor/discovery surface is gone (atoms 1-3). Scaffold SEEDING (the template-tree copy + the existing-vs-new conditional) is the NEXT atom (A3) — do NOT touch the scaffold or templates/workspace-cocoder/** here.

DO:
1. CREATE packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md with EXACTLY this content (you may fix obvious markdown formatting but preserve every substantive clause; do NOT add a DRAFT/inert marker — this priority is real):
---
id: onboard-existing
title: "Onboard an existing repo — deep multi-agent audit that authors its cocoder/ governance"
---

## Objective
An existing repo (real product code, no `cocoder/` governance yet) is onboarded into a CoCoder-style build through a **world-class, multi-pass code review + audit** that authors its governance — never one cheap pass. Oscar drives the audit through the ordinary Oscar<->Bob<->founder run loop: deep-read the repo, cross-check the reads adversarially, surface findings and questions to the founder, then draft the target's `cocoder/**` governance (memory/codebase map, architecture notes, candidate priorities with draft Objectives, persona/standards deltas). **The founder ratifies every drafted Objective before anything is runnable;** a first ordinary run then executes against ratified work.

**Verified when:** a real existing repo is onboarded end-to-end — scaffold (already present) -> deep-read + cross-check -> founder answers + ratifies the drafted Objectives -> a first ordinary run lands — with every audit finding traceable to repo reality (file:line evidence, not hallucinated).

**Trust boundary (hard):** the audit writes ONLY the target's `cocoder/**`; it reviews and proposes, and **never touches the user's product code**. Any code change is deferred to a separate, founder-ratified ordinary priority. This is the user's first interaction with CoCoder — it earns trust by proposing, not seizing. Enforced at the commit spine by the `cocoder/**` audit write-boundary (ADR-0023). Never the engine install.

This is the **big lift** — expensive by design (multi-agent, top-tier models).

## How Oscar runs this — the audit decomposition
Oscar decomposes the audit into ordinary atoms delegated to Bob (and adversarial orchestrator reads), reusing the audit engines as plain tooling. Founder gates are ordinary Oscar founder beats (surfaced conversationally and in the wrap), not frozen JSON gates.

1. **Scaffold** — the `cocoder/` zone already exists (seeded at workspace creation). No work.
2. **Recon + spend (founder beat).** Delegate a recon atom reusing `inventoryRepo` + `runAgenticRecon` + `runIntentIntake` + `buildEstimate`: map languages, packages, build/test commands, entry points, dep graph, size; capture intent from artifacts (README/docs/history) separated from founder-asserted intent; produce a per-phase cost/time estimate. **Surface the map + spend estimate to the founder and get approval before the expensive read.**
3. **Dual-source deep read.** For each subsystem, delegate TWO independent reads — Bob (builder) and an adversarial orchestrator read — reusing the `deep-read` Play + `runDeepReadSource`/`combineSourcePair`, top-tier and necessarily-different sources (ADR-0018). These are loop-shaped atoms whose scripted exit criteria are the deterministic caps (read-until-understood; 4 iterations / 45 min / token cap) per `loop-packets.md`. Disagreement between sources is signal, not noise.
4. **Adversarial cross-check (convergence).** Reuse the p3 cross-check (`buildRound`): round over the dual-source findings until no new contradiction or coverage gap surfaces (capped 3 rounds / 30 min / token cap); unresolved items are preserved, never silently dropped. A finding with no file:line evidence is treated as unverified.
5. **Founder questions (founder beat).** Surface the three classes from `buildFounderQuestions`: clarifications, conflicting/unresolved findings, and code issues that should become their own future priorities (the audit must NOT fix them itself). Founder answers feed synthesis.
6. **Synthesize.** Reuse `synthesizeP5Governance` to draft `cocoder/**` governance from VERIFIED findings + founder answers: `memory/` codebase map + tech stack, architecture notes, candidate priorities each with a draft Objective traceable to a finding, persona/standards deltas. Staged, not yet applied.
7. **Ratify (founder beat).** The founder ratifies/edits **each** drafted Objective through the ordinary verify/wrap path; reuse `applyP6Governance` to materialize the ratified governance into the target's `cocoder/**` through the commit spine WITH the `cocoder/**` audit write-boundary. **Nothing is runnable until ratified.**
8. **Prove.** Launch a first ordinary run against a ratified priority — the onboarding is real when that run lands.

## Quality is mechanism, not vibes
The dual-source fan-out (step 3, not one context window) + the convergence cross-check (step 4) are how the 'world-class' bar is met; the recon/spend, founder-questions, and ratify beats protect spend, truth, and trust. Every finding traces to file:line or it is unverified. Never accept agent confidence as fact (shared standards).
---END FILE CONTENT---

2. DELETE packages/personas/base/playbooks/cocoder-takeover.md (its substance now lives in the priority above).
3. UPDATE packages/personas/base/playbooks/README.md: remove/adjust the `cocoder-takeover` index row — onboard-existing is now an ordinary priority (packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md), no longer a base/playbooks meta-Playbook; and fix any 'inert until the loader reads base/playbooks/' wording that is now false (the loader was retired in atom 3). Leave the new-primary.md / drift-audit.md / new-primary-tech-stack.md skeleton BODIES in place (their migration to seeded priorities is future work) — only adjust their README index wording if it asserts the deleted loader.
4. UPDATE the LIVE cross-references to 'Takeover' in packages/personas/base/playbooks/new-primary.md and packages/personas/base/playbooks/new-primary-tech-stack.md: where they say to NOT import the 'Takeover' machinery, rename to 'the onboard-existing audit' / 'Onboard (existing repo)'. Only the live forward-looking cross-references — do NOT rewrite historical narrative.

DO NOT TOUCH: any code (packages/**/src, tests), the scaffold, templates/workspace-cocoder/**, cocoder/** (governance/ADRs/priorities are Oscar's), or the new-primary/drift skeleton bodies.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA / VERIFY (run and paste output):
- `grep -rn cocoder-takeover packages/personas` returns NOTHING (the id/file is gone from personas).
- packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md exists with the content above; it is a clean ordinary-priority shape (frontmatter id+title, ## Objective, the decomposition, the quality note); NO DRAFT/inert marker; NO `type: onboarding-playbook` / `mode: takeover` / `modelPin` frontmatter (those were retired loader concepts).
- `pnpm -w typecheck` clean and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` + `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` green (markdown-only change must not break anything; if a test referenced the deleted cocoder-takeover.md path, STOP and report — do not edit code to compensate).
- `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` green.
MUST NOT BREAK: no code references the deleted skeleton; the diff is confined to packages/personas/base/{priorities/onboard-existing.md (new), playbooks/cocoder-takeover.md (deleted), playbooks/README.md, playbooks/new-primary.md, playbooks/new-primary-tech-stack.md}. If anything forces a code or scaffold change, STOP and report.

data.reason: onboard-existing.md authored as a clean ordinary priority (Objective + 8-step engine-reusing decomposition + hard cocoder/**-only trust promise, no loader/mode concepts); cocoder-takeover.md deleted, gone from packages/personas; README + new-primary cross-refs renamed; diff confined to packages/personas markdown; typecheck + topology + core 367 + daemon 211 green. Remaining cocoder-takeover test-label string literals (commit-gate/read-surfaces) deliberately deferred to A3 boundary re-wiring.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 6 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md, cocoder/priorities/new-primary-root.md, cocoder/decisions/0020-primary-root-audit.md.
